items.title: Nuclear powers expand and renew their arsenals in a Cold War climate
Iran and US reach deal to end war , open Strait of Hormuz but challenges remain
U . S . and Iran announce an initial deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

During a meeting with President @ParmelinG upon arriving in Geneva, I thanked Switzerland for all the support it has provided and, in particular, for its readiness to help in bringing peace closer.

I informed Mr. President about Russia’s continued attacks and our needs to strengthen air defense. I spoke about the situation on the frontline and Ukraine’s long-range sanctions. Our mid-range strikes and deep strikes are quite rightly forcing Russia to feel the consequences of the war it unleashed. And we discussed which sanctions measures by our partners can also force Russia to the negotiating table.

We also spoke about cooperation in the energy sector. Switzerland helped us get through this winter, and we are counting on continued support. Thank you, Switzerland!

items.title: Will Commercial Ships Have to Pay to Pass Through the Strait of Hormuz? Maybe.
items.summary: Charging a toll is illegal under international law, but some fees are allowed for services. It is not clear what services Iran would provide, but there were no fees charged before the war.

items.title: UK charges Indian captain of suspected Russian shadow tanker seized in Channel
items.summary:
British prosecutors have charged with sanctions contravention offences the Indian captain of an interdicted alleged Russian shadow fleet vessel seized in the Channel, the National Crime Agency (NCA) said on Monday.

items.title: Gold Holds Gain as Trump Touts Reopening of Hormuz This Week

items.title: Oil Steadies Near Lowest Since March on Outlook for Hormuz Deal

items.title: Oil Holds Losses After Iran Deal Spurs Stock Rally: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Oil held losses after tumbling the most in more than two weeks on Monday as the US and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns over energy supplies and fueling a global equity rally. Stocks in Asia were set for a mixed open.

After US-Iran deal, Netanyahu says he will run in coming Israeli elections
items.summary:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday said he intends to run in elections scheduled for later this year, as he faces domestic criticism over his wartime leadership.
Netanyahu, 76, is Israel’s longest-serving prime minister, has led his country through three years of war on multiple interconnected fronts and is currently on trial for corruption.

items.title: Zelensky proposes meeting Putin in US after Russian leader ‘ignores’ G7 invitation
items.summary:
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Monday he was willing to meet Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin in the United States, suggesting Putin would find such an offer “harder to refuse”.
The Ukrainian leader said he made the proposal in a call with US President Donald Trump, and was waiting to see what came of it.
